# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.
# Category: cloud_database_security

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0
resources:
  - type: Microsoft.Sql/servers
    apiVersion: 2021-02-01
    name: staging-sqlserver
    location: [parameters('location')]
    properties:
      administratorLogin: sqladmin
      administratorLoginPassword: AzureSQL2024!
      version: '12.0'
      sslEnforcement: Enabled
      minimalTlsVersion: TLS1_2
    resources:
      - type: databases
        apiVersion: 2021-02-01
        name: staging-sqldatabase
        properties:
          edition: Standard
          requestedServiceObjective: S1
      - type: firewallRules
        apiVersion: 2021-02-01
        name: allowAllAzureServices
        properties:
          startIpAddress: 0.0.0.0
          endIpAddress: 0.0.0.0
      - type: firewallRules
        apiVersion: 2021-02-01
        name: devIpAddress1
        properties:
          startIpAddress: [parameters('devIp1')]
          endIpAddress: [parameters('devIp1')]
      - type: firewallRules
        apiVersion: 2021-02-01
        name: devIpAddress2
        properties:
          startIpAddress: [parameters('devIp2')]
          endIpAddress: [parameters('devIp2')]
        
parameters:
  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: eastus
  devIp1:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'XX.XX.XX.XX'  # Replace with your development IP
  devIp2:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 'YY.YY.YY.YY'  # Replace with your second development IP
